concerns.text: The random‑graph baseline (Phase 11) generates a degree‑preserving rewired network but then computes AUROC/AUPRC using the *same* raw correlation scores. Reusing unchanged predictor scores on a rewired edge set does not provide a proper null model; it mixes the original correlation information with a randomized topology, potentially inflating or deflating the baseline AUROC. A correct baseline should either (a) recompute correlation scores on the randomized network or (b) randomize the predictor scores (e.g., permutation of correlation values) before AUROC/AUPRC calculation, ensuring the baseline reflects a predictor with no true signal.
